title: Wall-Mounted Drop-Leaf Folding Table
summary: A space-saving table that folds flat against the wall, using half-lap and dowel-reinforced joints instead of hinge hardware alone.
description:
A folding table usually depends entirely on its hinges — when they wear out or rack sideways, the whole table gets wobbly. This one leans on its joinery instead: a half-lap wall bracket and a dowel-reinforced, notched leg assembly do the structural work, with hinges only handling the actual folding motion.

Folded flat, the whole assembly **projects less than 4" from the wall** — closer to a large picture frame than a piece of furniture — which is the entire point for a small kitchen, laundry room, or workshop that needs a work surface only some of the time.

Build and dry-fit the folding leg assembly **before** you mount anything to the wall. The notched joint that lets the legs fold flat has to swing cleanly through its full range on the bench; a binding joint is far easier to fix on sawhorses than after it's already anchored to studs.

tools.note: Cuts the half-lap joints in the wall bracket.
For the dowel-reinforced butt joints in the folding leg assembly.

materials.name: Hardwood board, 1x4, assorted lengths
materials.note: Wall bracket frame and folding leg assembly.

materials.name: Plywood or edge-glued hardwood panel, partial sheet
Piano hinge
materials.note: Lets the top swing up and fold down.

cutList.note: Get the angled notch that lets the assembly fold flat.

steps.title: Build the wall bracket frame
steps.body: Cut the wall bracket rails and side supports, then join them with half-lap joints to form a rigid frame that will anchor to the wall.

steps.title: Build the folding leg assembly
steps.body: Build the folding leg support assembly using dowel-reinforced butt joints and an angled notched joint that lets it fold flat. Dry-fit and swing it through its full range on the bench before moving on.

steps.title: Mount the wall bracket
steps.body: Mount the back frame to the wall with anchors and screws, into studs if at all possible given the load a tabletop puts on it.

steps.title: Attach the top and leg assembly
steps.body: Attach the folding top and leg assembly to the wall bracket with the piano hinge so it swings up to a level, supported position and folds flat against the wall when not needed.
materials: Plywood or edge-glued hardwood panel, partial sheet

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand and finish the whole assembly with stain or paint.
